title: Pipes & Grep
summary: Filter logs and connect commands through streams with grep and pipes.

steps.prompt: Open the access log and skim the lines.
steps.explanation: cat and head print file contents so you can see what you will filter.
steps.hint: Use cat or head on access.log.

steps.prompt: Filter the log for ERROR lines.
steps.explanation: grep scans each line and prints matches — perfect for finding errors in a log.
steps.hint: Use grep with the word ERROR and the log file name.

steps.prompt: Count how many ERROR lines appear in the log.
steps.explanation: Piping grep into wc -l counts matching lines; grep -c does it in one command.
steps.hint: Pipe grep output into wc -l, or use grep -c.
